title: Plaster Calculator
shortDescription: Calculate how much plaster you need for exterior facades or interior walls based on layer thickness.
metaTitle: Plaster Calculator | Bags, kg and Waste Estimate
metaDescription: Estimate plaster consumption in kg and how many bags you need for facade or interior plaster. Enter area, consumption per mm, thickness, waste and bag weight.
intro: Use this plaster calculator to quickly estimate plaster quantity for facade and interior wall work. Enter the area, material consumption per mm, layer thickness, waste percentage and bag weight to find the total kilograms and the number of bags needed.
overview: This tool helps you plan plaster orders more accurately for renovation and finishing work. It calculates the base plaster demand, adds your waste allowance and converts the result into bag counts. It works for common facade plaster and interior plaster planning where you need a fast answer to: how much plaster do I need?
howItWorks: Enter the surface area in square meters.
Set the plaster consumption per 1 mm in kg/m²/mm.
Provide the intended layer thickness in millimeters.
Add a waste percentage for cuts, losses or uneven surfaces.
Enter the bag weight in kilograms.
View the base kilograms, total kilograms with waste and the required number of bags.
useCases: Estimating facade plaster quantities before ordering materials.
Planning interior plaster for walls and ceilings.
Calculating how many bags of plaster are needed for a specific renovation area.
Adding waste margin for rough substrates or application losses.
Comparing material demand for different layer thicknesses.

examples.title: Sample calculation
examples.input: Area: 100 m², consumption: 1.6 kg/m²/mm, thickness: 10 mm, waste: 10%, bag weight: 25 kg
examples.output: Base plaster: 1600 kg, total plaster: 1760 kg, bags needed: 71.04 → 72 bags (rounded up)
examples.description: A practical example for planning facade or interior plaster orders.

examples.title: Quick ordering check
examples.input: Area: 45 m², consumption: 1.4 kg/m²/mm, thickness: 8 mm, waste: 5%, bag weight: 30 kg
examples.output: Base plaster: 504 kg, total plaster: 529.2 kg, bags needed: 17.64 → 18 bags (rounded up)
examples.description: Useful for deciding how many bags to buy before starting work.

faq.question: How do I calculate how much plaster I need?
faq.answer: Multiply area by consumption per mm and by layer thickness. Then add your waste percentage and divide by the bag weight to get the number of bags.

faq.question: Does this calculator work for facade plaster and interior plaster?
faq.answer: Yes. It is suitable for both facade plaster and interior plaster planning as long as you know the material consumption per mm.

faq.question: Why should I add waste?
faq.answer: Waste covers application losses, uneven surfaces, leftovers in containers and small calculation differences.

faq.question: Are the bags rounded up?
faq.answer: Yes, in practice you should round up to the next full bag so you do not run short on site.

faq.question: What if my plaster consumption is given per centimeter?
faq.answer: Convert it to per millimeter before using the calculator, or multiply the centimeter value by 10 to get kg/m²/mm.

seo.title: Plaster Calculator - Estimate kg and bags needed
seo.description: Free plaster calculator for facade and interior walls. Calculate plaster consumption, total kilograms and number of bags from area, thickness, waste and bag weight.
keywords: how much plaster do I need
